# Claude Code prompt — [Fix] Cutting sheet — hide Shift everywhere, hide Method except Saw Cutting

You are working on `GH-Mantova/ProjectOperations`. Read `CLAUDE.md` and follow it.

## Background

The Concrete Cutting sub-tab on Scope of Works currently shows columns that aren't relevant for some cutting types:

- **Shift** column is shown for ALL cutting categories. Owner: not needed anywhere — hide it throughout the cutting sheet.
- **Method** column (N/A / high-frequency / fuel) is shown for ALL cutting categories. Owner: only relevant for **Saw Cutting** — hide it for Core Holes, Grinding, and any other categories.

Additionally — confirm the "Copy from above" button behaviour on the cutting card: it currently only fires when L × H × Depth all have values. Owner confirmed this is **by design** (the button copies dimensional data from the row above; if there's no dimensional data there's nothing to copy). No code change for this part, but document the behaviour in the PR body so it's clear it was reviewed and accepted.

## Phase 0

- `git fetch origin`; branch off `main`: `fix/cutting-columns-polish`.

## Fix

Find the cutting-sheet component (search the repo for `ScopeCuttingSheet` or grep for `Shift` and `Method` in the tendering pages — likely `apps/web/src/pages/tendering/ScopeCuttingSheet.tsx` or a sibling).

1. **Remove the Shift column entirely.** Drop the column header, the cell renderer for each row, the column from any column-config / column-visibility array. Also drop the `shift` field from the cutting item form if it's no longer used. Verify the API model — if `shift` is still on `CuttingSheetItem` in Prisma it can stay (no migration needed); just don't render it. Confirm no other consumer reads it.

2. **Hide the Method column for non-Saw-Cutting categories.** The cutting category is likely a discriminator on the cutting item (look for `category` / `cuttingType` / `equipment` field). The Method column should render ONLY when the current row's category is "saw cutting" (case-insensitive). For Core Holes and any other category, the Method column is hidden — both the cell value AND ideally the column header if the entire table is filtered to one category at a time. If the table mixes categories in one view, render the Method header always but blank out the cell for non-saw-cutting rows.

## Tests

- Render the cutting sheet with a saw-cutting item, a core-hole item, and a grinding item. Confirm:
  - Shift column does not appear anywhere.
  - Method column shows method value for the saw-cutting row, blank/hidden for core-hole and grinding rows.
- §6 pre-PR CI checklist green.
